claim_boundary: The route proves only the declared UI states, tasks, paths, and current observed/implementation evidence; it does not certify unvisited behavior, future AI behavior, or visual design quality.
do_not_use_when: The work is visual styling only, non-UI, code-structure planning, or prose/label/API evidence is being substituted for UI behavior proof.

downstream_targets.condition: UI transition/capability/payload targets need semantic obligation, owner code, and test binding.
Browser/manual/transition evidence is large, slow, layered, stale-prone, background, or release-only.
Reviewed UI behavior must become implementation module/component ownership.

native_checks.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report
native_checks.summary: Run UI flow, visible-surface, operability, and implementation evidence model checks.

route_summary: Model UI behavior and prove observed surface, capability/task coverage, journeys, operability, implementation evidence, geometry, and transition scope.

source_requirements.summary: Existing/runnable UI claims require complete observed-surface and user-visible capability/output accounting.
Every reachable enabled action has a modeled event and current functional chain/click evidence, pure-UI disposition, or owned blindspot.
Model/design evidence cannot prove implemented/runnable completion; source alignment, recovery, human, and current evidence-kind gates remain explicit.

use_when: UI controls, displays, states, journeys, visible surface, capabilities, tasks, click-through, layout/responsiveness, or transition evidence are in scope.

workflow.summary: Declare greenfield, source_based, or mixed mode; product/user scope; source authority; and requested UI claim.
Inventory observed visible items, required capabilities/outputs, tasks, controls, displays, states, events, source mappings, and blindspots.
Build interaction/journey/structure/text/task contracts and current functional chains, implementation runs, geometry, and transition targets as required.
Run the UI Flow model and reject unowned enabled actions, missing recovery, stale/weak evidence, or design-only runnable claims.
Return current UI evidence and gaps; hand semantic transition cells and large evidence matrices to MTA/TestMesh.
